title: The Cupboard
content:
I know a little cupboard,
    With a teeny tiny key,
    And there's a jar of Lollypops
         For me, me, me.

    It has a little shelf, my dear,
    As dark as dark can be,
    And there's a dish of Banbury Cakes
         For me, me, me.

    I have a small fat grandmamma,
    With a very slippery knee,
    And she's the Keeper of the Cupboard
         With the key, key, key.

    And I'm very good, my dear,
    As good as good can be,
    There's Branbury Cakes, and Lollypops
         For me, me, me.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
